description:
You are maintaining a small collection of in-house shell utilities stored in
/home/user/scripts.  
Before publishing these scripts to your public Git repository you want to make
sure that no hard-coded credentials accidentally slip through.  
Run ONE terminal command that recursively scans the entire
/home/user/scripts directory for any line that contains the literal string
password= (case-insensitive) and write the findings to the plain-text file

    /home/user/security_scan.log

Requirements for /home/user/security_scan.log  
1. Overwrite the file if it already exists.  
2. The file must contain ONLY the lines produced by the scan, nothing else.  
3. Each result line must have the exact format

   <absolute path to file>:<line number>:<matched line>

   • The absolute path must start with /home/user/…  
   • <line number> must be the 1-based line index inside the file.  
   • <matched line> has to be the complete original source line exactly as it
     appears in the script (including any leading/trailing spaces or quotes).

4. Results must be sorted by file path, then by ascending line number inside
   each file (the tool you pick can do this automatically as long as the final
   ordering follows this rule).

5. No header, footer, or summary lines—only the raw matches as specified.

Example of the required line layout (illustrative—do not hard-code this):

/home/user/scripts/example.sh:42:db_password="s3cr3t"

A single well-constructed command is enough; do not create additional helper
scripts or manually edit the log.
truth:
Initial filesystem state
------------------------
Directory: /home/user/scripts

1. File: /home/user/scripts/backup.sh  
   Contents (line numbers added for clarity only; they are NOT in the file):
     1  #!/bin/bash
     2  # Daily backup script
     3  password="letmein"
     4  tar -czf /tmp/backup.tgz /var/data

2. File: /home/user/scripts/deploy.sh  
   Contents:
     1  #!/bin/bash
     2  # Deployment helper
     3  export DB_password="P@ssw0rd!"
     4  echo "Deployment complete"

3. File: /home/user/scripts/clean.sh  
   Contents:
     1  #!/bin/bash
     2  # Cleanup utility
     3  # (no secrets here)

Verification expectations
-------------------------
After the agent finishes, the file /home/user/security_scan.log must exist and
contain exactly the two lines (in this order, final newline after each line):

/home/user/scripts/backup.sh:3:password="letmein"
/home/user/scripts/deploy.sh:3:export DB_password="P@ssw0rd!"

No other files may be created or modified. The agent does not need root
privileges, and the working directory is arbitrary as long as the absolute
paths in the log are correct.
